title: Quweisna Archaeological Area
description: The most significant archaeological site in the Delta, containing a large necropolis, mausoleums, and a sacred falcon cemetery.

title: Falcon Necropolis
description: A unique section of the Quweisna site dedicated to the burial of sacred falcons (Horus), indicating the site's religious importance.

title: Mudbrick Mausoleums of Quweisna
description: A collection of ancient mausoleums built from mudbrick, reflecting different architectural styles over centuries of use.

title: Sidi Shebl Al-Aswad Mosque
description: A major Islamic shrine dedicated to Mohammed Ibn Al-Fadl (Sidi Shebl), containing his mausoleum. It is a site of significant pilgrimage.

title: Mausoleum of Sidi Shebl
description: The tomb of the martyr Sidi Shebl and his sisters, located within the mosque complex in El Shohada (City of Martyrs).

title: Al-Omari Mosque (Ashmoun)
description: One of the oldest mosques in the governorate, built on the ruins of an ancient Roman fortress.

title: Al-Abbasi Mosque
description: A prominent historical mosque in the capital, renovated during the reign of Khedive Abbas Helmy II.

title: Sidi Khamis Mosque
description: An important historic mosque located in the capital city, Shibin El Kom, known for its religious significance.

title: Mosque of Sidi Ibrahim
description: A revered mosque in the city of Minuf, dedicated to the local saint Sidi Ibrahim.

title: Palace of Abdel-Aziz Pasha Fahmy
description: The historic residence of the famous Egyptian politician and judge, now often used for cultural events.

title: Dar Kebira (The Great House)
description: A historic residence in Minuf representing traditional architecture of the region's notables.

title: Tell El-Bandaria
description: An archaeological mound containing remains of ancient settlements, located near Tala.

title: Tell Sirsna
description: An archaeological hill (tell) in El Shohada district with buried ancient structures.

title: Tell Zawiyet Razin
description: A significant tell containing layers of history from the Pharaonic to Roman periods.

title: Tell Al-Ahmar
description: An archaeological site known for finds of pottery and small artifacts.

title: Tell Nadir
description: Another archaeological mound in the governorate, part of the network of ancient settlements in the Delta.

title: Church of St. George (Mar Girgis)
description: One of the oldest Coptic churches in the region, located in the village of Tubar.

title: Monastery of Archangel Michael
description: A Coptic monastery located near Minuf, preserving Christian heritage in the governorate.

title: Denshway Museum
description: A national museum commemorating the 1906 Denshway Incident and the Egyptian resistance against British occupation.

title: Sadat Museum
description: A museum dedicated to the life of President Anwar El Sadat, located in his home village.

title: Pharaonic Obelisk of Shibin
description: Originally from Athribis (Benha), these granite obelisk fragments are displayed in Shibin El Kom.

title: Sidi Ezz El-Din Mosque
description: A historic mosque in Tala district, noted for its local architectural style.

title: Village of Sahragt Architectural Remains
description: Area containing remnants of older settlements and traditional baths.

title: Historic Gates of Ashmoun
description: Remains of the old gates that once protected the city of Ashmoun.

title: Al-Mahatta Mosque
description: A distinctive mosque located near the station area in Shibin El Kom.
